text.title: headless-cli-four-guards Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for headless-cli-four-guards as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using --dangerously-skip-permissions to "make it just work" in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Removes guard #2 (allowlist). The agent can run arbitrary tools — including destructive bash, git push, network calls. Use a narrow allowlist instead, even if you have to enumerate it explicitly.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defeats guard #3. The cap exists to bound cost; pick a value tied to a real workflow length (e.g. 15-30 for a single coding task) and fail loudly if hit.

failure-mode.description: Running the headless command inside a function that pipes JSON into stdin "just in case the agent reads it."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The CLI does not consume stdin in -p mode; piping unexpected content can change the harness's behaviour and is not part of the supported contract.
